methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Viridian Space is a pre-seed stage company in Space & Aerospace founded by Rostislav Spektor and Matthew Feldman, having raised $2M+. The comparable universe includes 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies in the sector, though direct peer valuation data at the pre-seed stage remains sparse.

Stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation anchor. Using total raised ($2M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for pre-seed to seed advancement yields $5M. This method is applicable given Viridian's clear stage positioning. Peer-median triangulation is not viable; while 23 same-sector peers exist, zero companies meet narrow filters for stage, adjacent stage, or comparable valuation tier, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring cannot be executed because Viridian's revenue is not disclosed, preventing P/S multiple application despite a 64.2x median across the 6 public Space & Aerospace names.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $4M–$6M, with a $5M median. This reflects a single applicable method and material uncertainty around true comparables. Key risks include stage-progression reliance on a single multiplier and the absence of private company valuation calibration in this emerging subsector. Revenue traction, technology differentiation, and founder track record would sharpen future estimates materially.
